title: New Testament Foundations — General Epistles and Revelation
subtitle: Perseverance, Warning, and the New Creation
description: A structured study of Hebrews through Revelation examining the superiority of Christ, the call to endurance, warnings against false teaching, and the final vision of God's new creation.
learning_outcomes: Explain the argument for Christ's superiority over Moses, Aaron, and the old covenant in Hebrews
Identify the warning passages in Hebrews and their function
Describe the relationship between faith and works in James
Explain the structure and major symbols of Revelation
Trace the theme of perseverance through suffering across the General Epistles

theological_framework.emphasis: Christ's finality and the church's endurance to the end

units.title: Hebrews
units.description: Christ superior to all — the better covenant and priestly work (Lessons 1–3)

units.title: John's Letters and Jude
units.description: Truth, love, and contending for the faith (Lessons 10–13)

units.title: Revelation
units.description: The Lamb's victory and the new creation (Lessons 14–19)

instructional_approach.session_structure: 7-segment lesson flow (orientation, reading, context, analysis, themes, question, close)
instructional_approach.knowledge_assessment: factual recall questions at lesson end

content_scope.subject: General Epistles and Revelation
content_scope.primary_texts: Hebrews, James, 1-2 Peter, 1-2-3 John, Jude, Revelation

questions: What OT figure does Hebrews present as the type of Christ's priesthood?
What does James say faith without works is?
What does Peter call Christians in 1 Peter 2:9?
What are the seven churches addressed in Revelation 1-3?
What does Revelation 21 describe as God's final act?

next_course.title: Old Testament Deep Study
next_course.description: Systematic study of the Old Testament from Genesis through Chronicles
